perturbation.original_text: Cards are intended for use in the United States and must be designated for receipt of shipment within the United States.
perturbation.changed_text: Cards are intended for use in the United States and must be designated for receipt of shipment within the United States. However, under special circumstances and with prior written approval from AEIS, Cards may be shipped internationally.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds the possibility of international shipments, which contradicts the original statement that the cards are designated for use and shipment within the United States. This creates uncertainty about the geographical limits of the agreement. The original text indicates exclusive use and shipment within the United States, while the addition allows for international shipment under certain, undefined circumstances. This creates ambiguity as to when and how international shipments are permitted, undermining the clarity of the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Cards are intended for use in the United States and must be designated for receipt of shipment within the United States. However, under special circumstances and with prior written approval from AEIS, Cards may be shipped internationally.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that cards are intended for use and shipment within the United States. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing international shipments under certain circumstances, which directly contradicts the initial restriction. This contradiction creates uncertainty and ambiguity about the permissible geographical scope of card usage and shipment, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, because the conflicting implications occur within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: The following pricing is effective with the Effective date of this Agreement through the term of the Agreement and is subject to change as detailed in the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The following pricing is effective with the Effective date of this Agreement through the term of the Agreement, and is subject to change at AEIS's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the reference to changes being detailed in the agreement, implying AEIS can alter pricing without adhering to previously agreed-upon conditions. This contradicts any sections that may specify conditions for price changes, creating uncertainty about Schoolpop's financial obligations. The original text, stated the prices are subject to change as detailed in the agreement, the modified text, states that the pricing can change at AEIS's discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction in the payment terms, and the original pricing.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
SCHOOLPOP CARD PRICING
The following pricing is effective with the Effective date of this Agreement through the term of the Agreement, and is subject to change at AEIS's sole discretion.
Off-face point value based on volume mix:
Persona Select 8%
Encompass Select 2%
Fill It Up 4%
Be My Guest 4%
Category Card 3% (Contingent upon achieving a four and one-half percent (4.5%) weighted average commission via the contracted merchant.)
Grocery Card 3% (Contingent upon achieving a four percent (4%) weighted average commission via the contracted merchant.)
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that pricing changes would be "detailed in the agreement." The changed text removes this condition, stating that pricing is "subject to change at AEIS's sole discretion." The contradicted text, which establishes specific pricing, is fundamentally undermined by the change because AEIS can now alter the pricing at will, contradicting the purpose of the initially agreed-upon pricing terms.

perturbation.original_text: Schoolpop shall have the exclusive right to resell Cards in the NPO Marketplace. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Schoolpop shall fund from AEIS a minimum of $52,000,000 of Cards (the "Guaranteed Minimum") in each Contract Year in order to maintain the exclusive right to sell Cards in the NPO Marketplace.
perturbation.changed_text: Schoolpop shall have the exclusive right to resell Cards in the NPO Marketplace. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Schoolpop shall use commercially reasonable efforts to fund from AEIS a minimum of $52,000,000 of Cards (the "Guaranteed Minimum") in each Contract Year in order to maintain the exclusive right to sell Cards in the NPO Marketplace.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "shall fund" to "shall use commercially reasonable efforts to fund" weakens Schoolpop's obligation to meet the Guaranteed Minimum. This creates an in-text contradiction, because now Schoolpop might not be legally bound to meet that threshold. While the original text guarantees AEIS a certain value, the modified text casts doubt on whether AEIS would be able to get the guaranteed revenue or value, and the conditions for a reasonable effort are not being listed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schoolpop shall have the exclusive right to resell Cards in the NPO Marketplace. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Schoolpop shall use commercially reasonable efforts to fund from AEIS a minimum of $52,000,000 of Cards (the "Guaranteed Minimum") in each Contract Year in order to maintain the exclusive right to sell Cards in the NPO Marketplace. A Contract Year shall be the period of August 1 through July 31 of the succeeding year. In any Contract Year in which Schoolpop fails to fund the Guaranteed Minimum, AEIS shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to (a) terminate the Agreement or (b) revoke the exclusivity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "Schoolpop shall fund from AEIS a minimum of $52,000,000 of Cards", which is a guaranteed obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to "Schoolpop shall use commercially reasonable efforts to fund from AEIS a minimum of $52,000,000 of Cards". The contradicted text states that "In any Contract Year in which Schoolpop fails to fund the Guaranteed Minimum, AEIS shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to (a) terminate the Agreement or (b) revoke the exclusivity." Because the changed text introduces the concept of 'commercially reasonable efforts,' Schoolpop might not be legally bound to meet the $52,000,000 minimum, contradicting the condition in the contradicted text that AEIS can terminate the agreement if the guaranteed minimum is not funded. The original condition of guaranteed funding is now uncertain, creating a strong contradiction.
